5:
Once the barrier is down, we will be free to cross the One River and reach Doma Castle. It is at this point that we must turn to our Confederate and
Kojin allies.

Once the barrier is down, we will be free to cross the One River and reach Doma Castle. It is at this point that we must turn to our Confederate and
Kojin allies.

25:
My lord, the castle is a sacred symbol─the very heart of our nation!
To destroy it is unthinkable, unconscionable─you cannot do this!

My lord, the castle is a sacred symbol─the very heart of our nation!
To destroy it is unthinkable, unconscionable─you cannot do this!

28:
Stone walls do not a nation make, my friend. Her people do.
And as long as we yet live, we can rebuild.

Stone walls do not a nation make, my friend. Her people do.
And as long as we yet live, we can rebuild.

33:
You're awake. I was just, um... Well, they threw me out, actually.
Told me I needed to get some sleep, ready for the big day.

You're awake. I was just, um... Well, they threw me out, actually.
Told me I needed to get some sleep, ready for the big day.

38:
Oh, but before you sleep, you should look in on Alphinaud and Alisaie.
Quietly, of course.

Oh, but before you sleep, you should look in on Alphinaud and Alisaie.
Quietly, of course.

67:
Ahh...war. To wield power, to make men kneel before you.
There is no better feeling.

Ahh...war. To wield power, to make men kneel before you.
There is no better feeling.

73:
Swiftly, brothers! Fall not behind!
Make an offering of their steel to Father Azim!

Swiftly, brothers! Fall not behind!
Make an offering of their steel to Father Azim!

76:
That it should fall so soon!
Truly, our allies seem determined to surpass each other!

That it should fall so soon!
Truly, our allies seem determined to surpass each other!

77:
The magitek field is down. I repeat, the magitek field is down!
Confederate vessels, you are clear to advance upon the enclave!

The magitek field is down. I repeat, the magitek field is down!
Confederate vessels, you are clear to advance upon the enclave!

84:
Th-The procedure was carried out as instructed by Lord Aulus.
By all indications...it was a success.

Th-The procedure was carried out as instructed by Lord Aulus.
By all indications...it was a success.

90:
But you have endured their worst and survived. You know their tricks.
They will not find a flower simply waiting to be plucked...

But you have endured their worst and survived. You know their tricks.
They will not find a flower simply waiting to be plucked...

91:
Have you aught to say for yourself?
For what you have done to our people─your people?

Have you aught to say for yourself?
For what you have done to our people─your people?

92:
My people, he says.
The precious lordling beloved by all come to confront the wicked witch.

My people, he says.
The precious lordling beloved by all come to confront the wicked witch.

94:
And so I was married to a vicious old drunk who beat me as he pleased, and when I pleaded for help, I was told to grin and bear it. For the family. For him. For everyone's sake. They pretended not to notice, but they knew.
They knew.

And so I was married to a vicious old drunk who beat me as he pleased, and when I pleaded for help, I was told to grin and bear it. For the family. For him. For everyone's sake. They pretended not to notice, but they knew.
They knew.

96:
And then my husband passed away one day. And so I was sold off yet again,
to pay his debts. But this time, this time, I found a way to live for myself.
To survive. As a spy for the Empire.

And then my husband passed away one day. And so I was sold off yet again,
to pay his debts. But this time, this time, I found a way to live for myself.
To survive. As a spy for the Empire.

97:
Oh, those were the days, when the scales first fell from my eyes. No longer would I be a slave to my parents or my husband or the pleasure house.
I would be free...and receive due compensation...

Oh, those were the days, when the scales first fell from my eyes. No longer would I be a slave to my parents or my husband or the pleasure house.
I would be free...and receive due compensation...

101:
There was nothing I would not do to feel that joy again! To bend this cruel, twisted world to my whims!
...Now, having borne witness to my life's work, have you aught to say to me?

There was nothing I would not do to feel that joy again! To bend this cruel, twisted world to my whims!
...Now, having borne witness to my life's work, have you aught to say to me?

109:
Do your duty. Deliver Lord Hien and the others to safety. Now, forthwith!
I shall manage on my own.

Do your duty. Deliver Lord Hien and the others to safety. Now, forthwith!
I shall manage on my own.

118:
Do not despair, my friend. Never before have I felt so alive.
My eyes unclouded, my purpose clear. This is right.

Do not despair, my friend. Never before have I felt so alive.
My eyes unclouded, my purpose clear. This is right.

145:
Forgive me. This is rather more daunting than I had anticipated.
But you are right, I must be strong. Wish me luck.

Forgive me. This is rather more daunting than I had anticipated.
But you are right, I must be strong. Wish me luck.

146:
Hah hah! Hearing you say that, I find my courage somehow renewed.
Much obliged.

Hah hah! Hearing you say that, I find my courage somehow renewed.
Much obliged.

190:
(-Imperial Tribunus Militum-)Aye. When you think of all the resources we poured into that hole.
And for what?

(-Imperial Tribunus Militum-)Aye. When you think of all the resources we poured into that hole.
And for what?

195:
It's true what they say. You do have the eyes of a monster.
A ravenous, insatiable fiend.

It's true what they say. You do have the eyes of a monster.
A ravenous, insatiable fiend.
